title: carex maritima subsp. setina
intro: Carex maritima subsp. setina, also known as sea sedge or sea club-rush, is a type of sedge plant that is native to coastal areas in Europe and North Africa. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Carex maritima subsp. setina include reducing inflammation, treating digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its usage and efficacy may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
